extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping dyspnea from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. dyspnea appears in only one rule, r1 (priority 100, the highest), whose action sets acuity to self_care (the LOWEST acuity). r1 fires only when stiff_neck, rash, focal_weakness, and dyspnea are all true.

Baseline (dyspnea = false): stiff_neck=true, rash=true, focal_weakness=true, dyspnea=false, others false. Matching rules: r4 stiff_neck->urgent (pri 2), r5 rash->emergent (pri 3), r7 focal_weakness->urgent (pri 2). r1 does NOT fire (needs dyspnea). Highest priority is r5 (pri 3) -> acuity = emergent.

Counterexample (dyspnea = true), same other inputs: stiff_neck=true, rash=true, focal_weakness=true, dyspnea=true, others false. Now r1 fires (all four conditions met) with priority 100, beating r5 (pri 3), r4/r7 (pri 2). Winner = r1 -> acuity = self_care.

Comparison: emergent (baseline, dyspnea false) -> self_care (dyspnea true). In the listed order [self_care < routine < urgent < emergent], self_care is strictly lower than emergent. Setting dyspnea true lowered acuity from emergent to self_care, violating monotonicity. The witness assignment lists the dyspnea=true input demonstrating the violation.
